title: Osteoarthritis (OA) — Knee & Hip
root_mechanism: Mechanical overload + chondrocyte senescence → proteoglycan loss (collagen type II degradation) → subchondral bone sclerosis → osteophyte formation → synovial inflammation (secondary) → cartilage thinning and joint space narrowing.

interventions.intervention: Hot paraffin wax bath on knee/hip or warm mustard oil joint pack
interventions.physiological_effect: Heat increases synovial fluid viscosity and distribution; reduces subchondral bone pain by dilating periarticular vasculature; relaxes periarticular muscle spasm

interventions.intervention: Alternating hot-cold pack on joint (2 min each × 5 cycles)
interventions.physiological_effect: Contrast therapy reduces secondary synovial oedema; vascular pumping flushes inflammatory metabolites from joint space
Reduces systemic IL-6 and TNF-α; reduces central sensitisation via limbic modulation

interventions.intervention: Collagen-rich anti-inflammatory: bone broth/egg white + vitamin C foods + turmeric
interventions.physiological_effect: Vitamin C essential for collagen type II synthesis in chondrocytes; turmeric curcumin inhibits MMP-3/13

interventions.intervention: Mahanarayan oil deep massage around (not on) joint + drainage technique
interventions.physiological_effect: Periarticular massage reduces muscle spasm and compensatory joint loading; oil reduces periarticular inflammation
Boswellic acid reduces cartilage-degrading leukotriene B4; guggulsterones reduce COX-2 in chondrocytes

interventions.intervention: Anti-inflammatory joint-supportive: selenium, zinc, omega-3 rich foods; avoid nightshades
interventions.physiological_effect: Selenium reduces oxidative damage to chondrocytes; zinc cofactor for SOD — chondrocyte antioxidant protection

interventions.intervention: Pool exercise or chair exercise (non-weight-bearing strengthening)
interventions.physiological_effect: Quadriceps strengthening reduces knee load by 4 kg per 1 kg muscle gained; water buoyancy prevents shear forces

interventions.intervention: Cold pack on swollen joint (if inflamed) or hot pack (if stiff, not inflamed)
interventions.physiological_effect: Acute oedema → cold reduces inflammatory exudate; chronic stiffness → heat increases collagen extensibility
Non-impact joint strengthening; periarticular muscle building; gentle ROM without cartilage compression

interventions.intervention: Epsom salt soak (full body or local knee/foot bath)
interventions.physiological_effect: Magnesium sulfate reduces periarticular muscle tension; sulfate is substrate for chondroitin sulfate synthesis

interventions.intervention: Dinner: bone broth soup + anti-inflammatory vegetables + small protein
interventions.physiological_effect: Evening bone broth provides glycine, proline, hydroxyproline — cartilage repair substrates during overnight regeneration

practices.maintenance_dose: If joint swells or pain > 5 VAS

relapse_rules.action: Phase 1 for 14 days; assess for aspiration

dietary_protocol.eat_window: 7:00 AM – 7:00 PM (regular 3 meals; cartilage repair requires consistent nutrient availability)
dietary_protocol.meal_order: Vitamin C foods first → Protein (collagen precursors) → Anti-inflammatory fats → Complex carbs

fasting_rules.specification: 1 cup warm bone broth — morning on waking or between meals
fasting_rules.physiological_effect: Provides glycine, proline, hydroxyproline — three amino acids in collagen type II; hydroxyproline directly incorporated into cartilage matrix during nocturnal repair

fasting_rules.specification: Collagen peptides (10 g) with vitamin C source — 30 min before exercise
fasting_rules.physiological_effect: Vitamin C obligatory cofactor for prolyl hydroxylase enzyme that cross-links collagen; before exercise maximises collagen deposition to loaded cartilage (AJSM 2019)

fasting_rules.specification: 400 mg extract — 20 min before lunch and dinner
fasting_rules.physiological_effect: AKBA inhibits MMP-3 and MMP-13 — pre-meal timing ensures therapeutic levels during post-meal inflammatory response

fasting_rules.specification: Eliminate tomato, eggplant for 6 weeks; reintroduce one at a time
fasting_rules.physiological_effect: Solanine alkaloids may inhibit cholinesterase → worsen joint pain in susceptible individuals

fasting_rules.specification: Caloric deficit of 500 kcal/day if BMI > 25
fasting_rules.physiological_effect: Every 1 kg weight loss reduces knee joint load by 4 kg; 5% body weight loss reduces OA pain ≥ 20%

phase_macros.carbohydrates: Moderate: 40% (anti-inflammatory whole grains; no refined carbs; no sugar)
phase_macros.protein: Moderate-high: 25–30% (collagen peptides + bone broth + fatty fish + eggs)
phase_macros.rationale: Maximise collagen synthesis substrates; minimise inflammatory eicosanoids; weight reduction via moderate caloric deficit

phase_macros.carbohydrates: Moderate: 45% (introduce more variety; glucosamine from bone broth)
phase_macros.rationale: Continued cartilage repair; quadriceps building via adequate protein; glucosamine from food sources supports proteoglycan synthesis
Lifelong anti-inflammatory diet; weight maintenance crucial; bone broth 3×/week maintained

items: Vitamin C: amla, guava, bell pepper (cooked)
Fish with skin — skin-on fatty fish
Trans fats and refined omega-6 oils
Fried foods (trans fats worsen synovial inflammation)
Large portions (weight gain = joint load amplification)
White flour products (AGEs damage cartilage collagen)

meals.what_to_eat: 1 cup warm bone broth + amla juice (30 mL) or lemon water
meals.physiological_rationale: Bone broth hydroxyproline absorbed first — maximises availability for cartilage repair; amla vitamin C activates collagen synthesis enzymes

meals.what_to_eat: Warm joint pack (20 min) then contrast therapy
meals.physiological_rationale: Thermal therapy before weight-bearing reduces stiffness; contrast therapy flushes overnight inflammatory metabolites

meals.what_to_eat: 2 eggs (ghee) + broccoli (cooked) + amla or kiwi (vitamin C) + turmeric milk (no sugar)
meals.physiological_rationale: Eggs: collagen precursors; broccoli sulforaphane blocks MMP enzymes; vitamin C enables collagen cross-linking

meals.what_to_eat: Collagen peptides (10 g) in warm water with lemon — 30 min before exercise
meals.physiological_rationale: Pre-exercise collagen + vitamin C doubles cartilage collagen deposition at loaded joints (AJSM 2019 RCT)
AKBA peaks at joint tissue 20 min post-ingestion; inhibits MMP enzymes during afternoon cartilage stress

meals.what_to_eat: Grilled salmon (150 g) + olive oil salad (leafy greens, broccoli, pomegranate) + small ragi or brown rice + curd
meals.physiological_rationale: Salmon omega-3 reduces synovial PGE2; olive oil oleocanthal = COX inhibitor; ragi calcium for subchondral bone

meals.what_to_eat: Tart cherry juice (150 mL) or pomegranate juice (100 mL) + 10 walnuts
meals.physiological_rationale: Tart cherry anthocyanins inhibit COX-1/2; walnuts ALA omega-3; before yoga reduces exercise-induced joint inflammation

meals.what_to_eat: Bone broth soup (1 large bowl) + steamed non-starchy vegetables + small fish or paneer + turmeric + 1 tsp ghee
meals.physiological_rationale: Second bone broth dose: overnight cartilage repair substrates; ghee butyrate repairs gut lining (gut-joint axis)

functional_foods.name: Bone broth
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Provides glycine, proline, hydroxyproline — collagen type II amino acids; glucosamine and chondroitin from cartilage remnants; gelatin seals gut lining

functional_foods.name: Collagen peptides (hydrolysed)
functional_foods.dose_timing: 10 g in warm water + vitamin C — 30 min before exercise daily
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Hydroxyproline dipeptides (Pro-Hyp, Hyp-Gly) directly stimulate chondrocyte collagen II synthesis; pre-exercise timing doubles deposition (AJSM RCT 2019)

functional_foods.name: Boswellia (Shallaki)
functional_foods.dose_timing: 400 mg standardised 65% boswellic acid — 20 min before meals, 2×/day; supervised
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: AKBA inhibits MMP-3 and MMP-13 (collagen-degrading enzymes); reduces WOMAC pain score ≥ 40% in 8 weeks (multiple RCTs)

functional_foods.name: Guggul (Commiphora mukul)
functional_foods.dose_timing: 250 mg guggulsterone equivalent — with lunch, supervised
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Guggulsterones block NF-kB and reduce COX-2 in chondrocytes; anti-inflammatory synergist with Boswellia

functional_foods.name: Turmeric + black pepper
functional_foods.dose_timing: 1 tsp curcumin + 1/4 tsp black pepper with fat — daily
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Curcumin inhibits MMP-3/13; reduces chondrocyte apoptosis; reduces synovial IL-1β and TNF-α

functional_foods.name: Pomegranate juice
functional_foods.dose_timing: 100–150 mL fresh pomegranate juice — daily afternoon
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Ellagic acid and punicalagins inhibit MMP-3 — primary enzyme degrading type II collagen in OA cartilage

functional_foods.name: Ginger (fresh)
functional_foods.dose_timing: 1-inch in decoction 3×/day; 1 tsp powder in food daily
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Inhibits prostaglandin E2 and leukotriene B4; reduces substance P in joint; equivalent to ibuprofen in knee OA (2-year RCT)

functional_foods.name: Mahanarayan oil
functional_foods.dose_timing: Warm massage around joint (not on painful area) — 15–20 min daily Phase 1
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Sesame base carries 50+ herbs transdermally; reduces periarticular muscle spasm; traditional reduction in WOMAC scores documented

asanas.physiological_effect: Strengthens quadriceps without joint compression; improves posture reducing compressive joint forces
Decompresses knee joint; reduces intra-articular pressure; gravity-assisted lymphatic drainage
Glute and hamstring strengthening reduces knee valgus stress; improves hip extension
Gentle hamstring stretch reduces posterior knee tension; improves knee extension ROM
Hip abductor strengthening without joint loading; reduces lateral knee pain
Calf strengthening improves ankle-knee chain mechanics; reduces ankle pronation
Pain modulation via endorphin release; reduces central sensitisation in OA
Reduces knee oedema; pain-free recovery position; synovial fluid redistribution

red_flags.sign: Sudden joint locking or giving way
red_flags.action: Rule out meniscal tear or loose body — urgent orthopaedic review

red_flags.sign: Hot, red, swollen joint with fever
red_flags.action: Rule out septic arthritis — emergency joint aspiration

red_flags.sign: Rapidly progressive joint space loss on X-ray

red_flags.action: Rule out nerve entrapment or vascular compromise

clinical_disclaimer: This protocol is for use under qualified BNYS clinical supervision. Every 1 kg weight loss reduces knee joint load by 4 kg — weight reduction is the single most impactful intervention. Boswellia requires clinician approval with anticoagulants. This document is for clinical reference only.
